response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and systems software engineer with a strong web, mobile, and low-latency application background, but his profile does not align with the Principal Performance Architect role. The position demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design for AI accelerators, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD architectures, and deep neural network workload optimization — none of which appear in his resume. His 20+ years of experience satisfy the education/experience threshold, and his work at Sociomantic on low-latency high-throughput systems shows some performance engineering instinct, but this is fundamentally a hardware architecture role and the candidate's entire career has been in software.
strengths: 20+ years of continuous professional software engineering experience across diverse domains (web, embedded, mobile, low-latency systems), satisfying the Bachelor's + 8yr education requirement
Demonstrated performance engineering mindset from high-throughput, low-latency work at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second using async I/O and high-performance databases
Breadth of systems exposure including embedded systems, robotic arms, and native app development shows ability to work across layers of the stack
concerns: Zero evidence of SoC or IP modeling experience, which is a core required skill for this role
No background in GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD architectures, or AI accelerator hardware — all of which are central to the job description
No deep learning or neural network workload analysis experience; AI mentions are limited to high-level agentic tooling (MCP frameworks), not hardware-level ML optimization

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware design experience
No AI/ML hardware or accelerator workload expertise
Strong low-latency systems and performance engineering history
